query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Elixir', 'A liquid which converts lead to gold', 'Critics argued that the plan might elixir the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elliptical', 'In a shape reminding of an ellipse, oval', 'Her elliptical became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elocution', 'The skill of a clear and expressive speech', 'Her elocution became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eloquence', 'The quality of artistry and persuasiveness in language', 'Scholars noted a striking eloquence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eloquent', 'Fluent and persuasive in speech or writing', '“The senator gave an eloquent speech about equality.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elucidate', 'To explain or clarify', '“The professor elucidated a difficult concept in simple terms.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elude', 'To evade, or escape from someone or something through skill', 'The author hoped the new policy would elude tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elusion', 'The act, or abstract properties, of eluding', 'Scholars noted a striking elusion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Elusive', 'Difficult to find or define', 'Over time, the community learned to elusive its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emaciat', 'Abnormally thin or weak', 'Her emaciat became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emaciate', 'To make extremely thin or wasted', 'Critics argued that the plan might emaciate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emaciated', 'Abnormally thin or weak', 'The passage presents a emaciated portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emanate', 'To come from a source, stem out of', 'Critics argued that the plan might emanate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emancipate', 'To set free from the power of another, to liberate, as:', 'Critics argued that the plan might emancipate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embargo', 'Any restriction placed on commerce by a government', 'The essay highlights the embargo of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embark', 'To get on a boat or ship or (outside the usa) an aeroplane', 'Over time, the community learned to embark its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embarrass', 'To cause confusion and shame to, disconcert, abash', 'Over time, the community learned to embarrass its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embellish', 'To make more attractive by adding details', 'Over time, the community learned to embellish its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embezzle', 'To steal or misappropriate money one has been trusted with', 'Critics argued that the plan might embezzle the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emblazon', 'To adorn with prominent markings', 'Critics argued that the plan might emblazon the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emblem', 'A representative symbol, such as a trademark or logo', 'Her emblem became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embodiment', 'A tangible or visible form of an idea, quality, or feeling', 'Her embodiment became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embody', 'To represent in a physical form, to incarnate or personify', 'Over time, the community learned to embody its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embolden', 'To render (someone) more bold or courageous', 'Critics argued that the plan might embolden the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embolism', 'An obstruction or occlusion of an artery', 'Her embolism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Embroil', 'To draw into a situation, to cause to be involved', 'Over time, the community learned to embroil its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emend', 'Make corrections and improvements to a text', 'The author hoped the new policy would emend tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emerge', 'To come into view', 'The author hoped the new policy would emerge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emergence', 'The act of coming forth from envelopment or concealment', 'The essay highlights the emergence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emergent', 'In the process', 'The reviewer described the tone as emergent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emeritus', 'Having retired but retaining his title as an honor', 'The passage presents a emeritus portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emigrant', 'Someone who leaves a country to settle in a new country', 'The author hoped the new policy would emigrant tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emigrate', 'To leave the country in which one lives and settle elsewhere', 'The author hoped the new policy would emigrate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eminence', 'Someone of high rank, reputation or social station', 'Scholars noted a striking eminence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Eminent', 'Famous and respected in a field', '“She is an eminent scientist in the field of genetics.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emissary', 'A person sent on a special mission', 'Her emissary became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emissivity', 'The ability of a material to emit energy as radiation', '“The spacecraft’s heat shield has high emissivity.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emit', 'To send out or give off', 'The author hoped the new policy would emit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emollient', 'Having the quality of softening or soothing the skin', 'What seemed emollient at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emolument', 'Payment for an office or employment, compensation for a job', 'The reviewer described the tone as emolument, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emote', 'To display emotions openly, especially while acting', 'The author hoped the new policy would emote tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Empathy', 'The ability to understand and share the feelings of others', 'Over time, the community learned to empathy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emphasis', 'Special weight given to something considered important', 'The author hoped the new policy would emphasis tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emphasize', 'To give special importance or stress to something', '“The teacher emphasized the importance of critical thinking.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emphatic', 'Expressing something forcibly and clearly', 'Her emphatic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Empirical', 'Based on observation or experience rather than theory', '“Empirical data is crucial for scientific research.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emporium', 'A market place or trading centre, particularly in antiquity', 'Scholars noted a striking emporium between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Empower', 'To give permission or power to do something', 'Critics argued that the plan might empower the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Emulate', 'To imitate or strive to equal or surpass', 'Over time, the community learned to emulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Enact', 'To make (a bill) into law', 'Critics argued that the plan might enact the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
